claim_text: Section averaging does not determine a unique one-dimensional model; the structural simplification used for vessel compliance changes the reduced differential operator.
revision_status: active in continuum and hierarchy prose

claim_text: Zero-dimensional and one-dimensional formulations retain different system information: 0D models represent global pressure-flow interactions, whereas 1D models additionally resolve distributed pulse-wave transmission.
revision_status: active in hierarchy and synthesis prose

claim_text: Boundary data may themselves be reduced models of omitted circulation; geometrical multiscale formulations couple local PDE problems to systemic lumped models through compatible interface variables.
revision_status: active in hierarchy, closures, and synthesis prose

claim_text: The reported 1D-3D FFR agreement was obtained under matched pressure boundary conditions and dedicated 1D stenosis and bifurcation loss terms, supporting an FFR-specific matched-contract comparison rather than model-tier equivalence.
revision_status: active in hierarchy, observables, and synthesis prose

claim_text: In one patient-specific intracranial stenosis study, rheology choice had negligible influence on translesional pressure ratio but observable influence in low-WSS regions, especially during diastole.
revision_status: active in closures and synthesis prose

claim_text: The WSS convention is part of the observation operator; distinct traction-projection formulas can materially change AWSS and OSI values in critical wall regions.
revision_status: active in continuum, hierarchy, and closures prose

claim_text: Geometry reconstruction is part of model uncertainty; in one aneurysm case, perturbations within imaging resolution produced sensitivity greater than but comparable to rheology and WSS differences of 3-18%.
revision_status: active in geometry and uncertainty prose

claim_text: The Boileau benchmark supplies shared cases for comparing six 1D schemes under declared conditions; it supports numerical comparison and reproducibility rather than universal stenosis accuracy.
revision_status: active in numerical evidence and synthesis prose

claim_text: A well-balanced claim must identify the preserved stationary set; the 2023 Pimentel-Garcia et al. method targets all stationary solutions of a model with discontinuous geometrical and mechanical properties.
scope_or_population: 1D blood flow with discontinuous geometrical and mechanical properties
revision_status: active in numerical methods and synthesis prose

claim_text: Verification, validation, and uncertainty quantification support different credibility claims: verification concerns mathematical and numerical realization, validation concerns a specified real application and observable, and UQ concerns effects of uncertain inputs and numerical quantities.
revision_status: active in numerical methods and conclusion prose

claim_text: Practical identifiability is data- and design-dependent; changing measured outputs and boundary-condition class changed which parameters could be inferred in a 1D pulmonary model.
revision_status: active in synthesis and conclusion prose

claim_text: Within its idealized stenosis and waveform study, the PCNDE model reported lower error than a specified 1D FEM baseline and sub-1.2% relative error against averaged 3D data; this is a conditional dataset-level result.
scope_or_population: idealized stenosis and waveform train/test setting

claim_text: The multifidelity operator model learned pressure and flow maps and then derived FFR within a simplified stenosis setting; its predictive scope is bounded by the geometry family, input space, data fidelities, and pressure-derived target.
scope_or_population: simplified stenosis setting with 201 training and 68 test cases

claim_text: The Canic et al. arXiv preprint extends 1D stenosis equations to account for longitudinally variable radius and reconstructs radial velocity in post-processing; the reconstructed field is not a native 1D state.
revision_status: active in 1D hierarchy and case-study prose

claim_text: openBF is useful as a reproducibility and software-comparison reference for an open finite-volume 1D workflow; availability alone does not validate a hemodynamic prediction.
revision_status: active in numerical methods and synthesis prose
